body:
# Can A server-side web component system for Crystal. Write components as HTML; render them to a string. Each component's `

{title}

<.slot/>

Welcome.

 ``` ## Installation Add to your `shard.yml`: ```yaml dependencies: can: github: trans/can ``` Then `shards install`. ## Quick start Templates live in `.can` files. A small Crystal class loads them at compile time and renders to an `IO`: ```crystal require "can" class HomePage Can.template "templates/components.can" # class scope: defines methods def render(io : IO) name = "Thomas" Can.template "templates/home.can" # method scope: emits HTML end end io = IO::Memory.new HomePage.new.render(io) puts io.to_s ``` Run with `crystal run`. The template is parsed and compiled to Crystal source at compile time — there's no runtime parsing, and template errors surface as compile errors. ## Interpolation `{expr}` evaluates a Crystal expression and HTML-escapes the result: ```html

Hi, {user.name}!

You have {todos.size} things to do.

``` For pre-sanitized content, use `<.raw>`: ```html
<.raw>{markdown_to_html(post.body)}
``` Or mark the value at the data layer: ```crystal trusted = Can.raw("safe") ``` ```html
{trusted}
``` Both compose. `<.raw>` doesn't penetrate into nested `<.def>` bodies — those have their own escape context. ## Special forms Dotted tags are language built-ins: | Form | Purpose | |---|---| | `<.def tag="…" param:foo="T">…` | Define a component. | | `<.if cond={…}>…` (with `<.elseif/>` / `<.else/>`) | Conditional. | | `<.for each={x in xs}>…` | Iteration. | | `<.let name="x" value={…}>…` | Local binding for the body. | | `<.slot/>` / `<.slot name="…"/>` | Slot placeholder in a component body. | | `<.require from="…"/>` | `require` another Crystal file. | | `<.raw>…` | No-escape zone. | `:if` and `:for` are attribute-form shortcuts: ```html

secret

  • {item}
    • ``` When both are present, `:if` is outer (same as Vue 3). `<.if>` supports `<.elseif/>` and `<.else/>` sentinels: ```html <.if cond={n == 0}> zero <.elseif cond={n < 10}/> small <.else/> large ``` Codegen collapses these into a Crystal `if/elsif/elsif/else/end` chain. ## Components A `<.def>` defines a component. Invoke it as a tag — the tag name maps to a Crystal method: - ``, ``, and `` all map to method `card`. - Hyphens become underscores; PascalCase becomes snake_case. - Real HTML element names (`a`, `div`, `h1`, …) render literally. ```html <.def tag="badge" param:label="String" param:emoji={"✨"}> {emoji} {label} ``` ### Params `param:foo="T"` declares a required param of type `T`. `param:foo={value}` declares an optional one — the default is the given Crystal expression and the type is inferred from the literal: ```html <.def tag="row" param:label="String" ← required param:n={0_i32} ← Int32, default 0 param:items={[] of String} ← Array(String), default [] > … ``` Crystal's typed literal suffixes (`_i32`, `_u8`, `_f64`, …) cover the cases where bare numerics would be ambiguous. ### Slots A `<.slot/>` in a component body marks where the invocation's children render. Named slots use `<.slot name="…"/>` in the def and `<:name>…` at the call site: ```html <.def tag="layout" param:heading="String">

    {heading}

    <.slot name="nav"/>
    <.slot/>
    <.slot name="footer"/>
    <:nav>home

    page content

    <:footer>© 2026     ``` Slot fill content runs in the *caller's* scope and captures the caller's local variables. ### Top-level vs inline defs A `<.def>` at the top of a template loaded at class scope becomes a real method on the surrounding class. A `<.def>` inside another element — or inside a template loaded inside a method — becomes a local `Proc` that captures surrounding bindings: ```html
    <.def tag="pill">[{label}] <.for each={label in tags}>
    ``` Inline defs **can't host slots or have param defaults** (Crystal `Proc`s don't support either). Put slot-bearing or default-bearing components in a separate `.can` file loaded at class scope: ```crystal class Page Can.template "components.can" # defs with slots/defaults here def render(io : IO) Can.template "page.can" # rendering content here end end ``` A clear compile-time error fires if you violate this. ## CSS scoping A `

    {title}

    ``` Browser receives (with `id` like `a3f9`): ```html

    Hello

    ``` The scoper recurses into `@media` and `@supports`, passes `@keyframes` / `@font-face` / `@import` through unchanged, and respects comments and strings. Components without a `

class_methods.name: raw
class_methods.doc:
Wraps content as trusted/pre-escaped. Composes with `<.raw>` — both end
up writing the string verbatim.

class_methods.name: write_escaped
class_methods.doc:
Runtime escape used by codegen for every non-raw `{expr}`. Skips
HTML-escaping when the value is already a `SafeString`.
class_methods.summary: Runtime escape used by codegen for every non-raw {expr}.

def.name: write_escaped
def.body:
if value.is_a?(SafeString)
  io << value.value
else
  io << (::HTML.escape(value.to_s))
end

macros.name: template
macros.doc:
Same as `template_inline`, but reads the template from a file path
resolved relative to the compile-time working directory (typically the
project root).

    io = IO::Memory.new
    user = current_user
    Can.template "pages/home.can"

When called inside a method body, top-level `<.def>` blocks are lowered
to local `Proc`s (since Crystal forbids nested `def`s). Slot-bearing
components must therefore be defined at class/module scope.
macros.summary: Same as template_inline, but reads the template from a file path resolved relative to the compile-time working directory (typically the project root).

macros.name: template_inline
macros.doc:
Compiles a template literal at macro expansion time and splices the generated Crystal source in-place. The generated code writes HTML to a local `io` (which the caller is responsible for having in scope). io = IO::Memory.new name = "Thomas" Can.template_inline "

Hello, {name}!

" io.to_s # => "

Hello, Thomas!

"
macros.summary: Compiles a template literal at macro expansion time and splices the generated Crystal source in-place.

def.name: new
def.body:
_ = allocate
_.initialize(tag, params, body, line, column)
if _.responds_to?(:finalize)
  ::GC.add_finalizer(_)
end

types.name: ElseMark
types.doc:
Transient parser-only nodes: `<.else/>` and `<.elseif cond={…}/>` are
sentinels emitted by the parser and consumed by `build_if`, which
restructures the body into nested `If` nodes. They never reach codegen
in well-formed templates; if they do, codegen raises a "stray" error.
types.summary: Transient parser-only nodes: <.else/> and <.elseif cond={…}/> are sentinels emitted by the parser and consumed by build_if, which restructures the body into nested If nodes.

types.name: ExprAttr
types.doc: items={list} — a single Crystal expression, not a string
types.summary: items={list} — a single Crystal expression, not a string

types.name: Raw
types.doc:
<.raw>…</.raw> — every {expr} interpolation directly inside this body
is emitted verbatim (no HTML escape). Doesn't penetrate into <.def>
bodies nested within (those have their own escape context).
types.summary: <.raw>…</.raw> — every {expr} interpolation directly inside this body is emitted verbatim (no HTML escape).

types.name: SlotFill
types.doc:
<:name>…</:name> — fills a named slot at a component invocation site.
Parsed for any tag starting with `:`. Only meaningful as a child of a
component-invocation Element; codegen rejects it elsewhere.
types.summary: <:name>…</:name> — fills a named slot at a component invocation site.

types.name: Codegen
types.doc:
Codegen: emits Crystal source that writes HTML to a local `io`.

Static text from the source template is emitted verbatim — author
responsibility to write valid HTML (e.g. `<` for literal `<`).
Interpolated `{expr}` values are HTML-escaped at runtime via stdlib
`::HTML.escape`.

An element whose tag is in `HTML_ELEMENTS` renders as literal HTML.
Any other tag is treated as a component invocation: `<Card title="…">…</Card>`
becomes `card(io, title: "…") do |io| … end`. The tag is mapped to a
method name via `tag.gsub('-', '_').underscore`.

`<.def>` at the top level becomes a method (with optional named-slot
`Proc` params and a default-slot `&block`). `<.def>` inside a body
becomes a local `Proc` that closes over surrounding bindings; v1
restriction: inline defs can't host `<.slot/>` and can't be invoked
with slot content.
types.summary: Codegen: emits Crystal source that writes HTML to a local io.

constructors.name: new
constructors.args_html: (out __arg0 : IO, scope : Symbol = :class)

types.name: CssScoper
types.summary: Rewrites CSS to scope every selector to a given attribute.

constructors.name: new
constructors.args_html: (msg : String, line : Int32 = 0, column : Int32 = 0)

types.name: Parser
types.doc:
Strict XML-ish parser for the can template language. Tags must be
explicitly closed (or self-closed); attribute values must be quoted
(`"..."` / `'...'`) or expression-form (`={...}`).

`<style>` and `<script>` switch to raw-text mode — content is captured
verbatim until the matching close tag, no nested-tag or interpolation
parsing. (Trade-off: no `{expr}` inside CSS for now; revisit later.)
types.summary: Strict XML-ish parser for the can template language.

constructors.name: new
constructors.args_html: (source : String)

def.name: parse
def.body:
l, c = @line, @col
children = parse_nodes(until_close: nil)
AST::Template.new(children, l, c)

types.name: SafeString
types.doc:
Marks a string as pre-escaped/trusted. Interpolations that evaluate to a
`SafeString` are emitted verbatim instead of HTML-escaped.

constructors.name: new
constructors.args_html: (value : String)

instance_methods.name: to_s
instance_methods.doc:
Returns a nicely readable and concise string representation of this object,
typically intended for users.

This method should usually **not** be overridden. It delegates to
`#to_s(IO)` which can be overridden for custom implementations.

Also see `#inspect`.
instance_methods.summary: Returns a nicely readable and concise string representation of this object, typically intended for users.
